manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: pit, hand, comic, fence, mint, chest, olympus, cold, stadium.
Your opponent's team words are: amazon, kid, bolt, ground, post, skyscraper, horseshoe, green.
Distractor words are: knife, crown, head, ship, slip, rock, cricket.
The kill words are: alien.

TARGETS: cold, chest, mint

    *   Kill Word: alien.
    *   Output Format: Strict format required (`CLUE: <WORD>\nTARGETS: <TARGETS>`). No extra text.
    *   Constraint: Clue must be semantically related, cannot be on the board or contain parts of the target words.

2.  **Evaluate My Team Words:**
    *   pit, hand, comic, fence, mint, chest, olympus, cold, stadium.

3.  **Find Associations:**

    *   *hand*: handball, handshake, handful, left/right, body part.
    *   *comic*: book, strip, marvel, dc, funny, superhero.
    *   *fence*: picket, white, border, enclosure, fencing (sport).
    *   *mint*: candy, money, fresh, plant, condition (mint condition).
    *   *chest*: body part, treasure, box, lungs, hair.
    *   *olympus*: mount, greek, gods, zeus.
    *   *cold*: temperature, flu, winter, ice, hot/cold.
    *   *stadium*: sports, arena, football, seats, crowd.

4.  **Identify Potential Groups:**
    *   *Body Parts*: hand, chest. (pit? armpit. head is distractor).
    *   *Sports/Venues*: stadium, olympus (olympics?), pit (racing pit?), fence (fencing?).

reasoning_details.text:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Task: Provide a single-word clue for a Codenames-style game.
    *   Input: Lists of words for my team, opponent's team, distractors, and kill words.
    *   My Team Words: pit, hand, comic, fence, mint, chest, olympus, cold, stadium.
    *   Opponent Words: amazon, kid, bolt, ground, post, skyscraper, horseshoe, green.
    *   Distractor Words: knife, crown, head, ship, slip, rock, cricket.
    *   Kill Word: alien.
    *   Output Format: Strict format required (`CLUE: <WORD>\nTARGETS: <TARGETS>`). No extra text.
    *   Constraint: Clue must be semantically related, cannot be on the board or contain parts of the target words.

2.  **Evaluate My Team Words:**
    *   pit, hand, comic, fence, mint, chest, olympus, cold, stadium.

3.  **Find Associations:**
    *   *pit*: snake pit, pit bull, armpit, mining, stomach.
    *   *hand*: handball, handshake, handful, left/right, body part.
    *   *comic*: book, strip, marvel, dc, funny, superhero.
    *   *fence*: picket, white, border, enclosure, fencing (sport).
    *   *mint*: candy, money, fresh, plant, condition (mint condition).
    *   *chest*: body part, treasure, box, lungs, hair.
    *   *olympus*: mount, greek, gods, zeus.
    *   *cold*: temperature, flu, winter, ice, hot/cold.
    *   *stadium*: sports, arena, football, seats, crowd.

4.  **Identify Potential Groups:**

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: pit, hand, comic, fence, mint, chest, olympus, cold, stadium.
Your opponent's team words are: amazon, kid, bolt, ground, post, skyscraper, horseshoe, green.
Distractor words are: knife, crown, head, ship, slip, rock, cricket.
The kill words are: alien.

CLUE: HEALTH
TARGETS: cold, chest, mint

The word cold was guessed correctly. The word head was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: pit, hand, comic, fence, mint, chest, olympus, stadium.
Remaining words for your opponent are: amazon, kid, bolt, ground, post, skyscraper, horseshoe, green.
Remaining distractor words are: knife, crown, ship, slip, rock, cricket.
Remaining kill words are: alien.

reasoning_details.text:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   Game: Codenames (or similar word association game).
    *   Role: Spymaster giving a clue.
    *   Input: Remaining team words, opponent words, distractor words, kill words.
    *   Constraint: Single-word clue, comma-separated list of target words.
    *   Format:
        CLUE: <WORD>
        TARGETS: <TARGETS>
    *   Goal: Connect multiple team words without touching opponent/distractor/kill words.
    *   Current State: 'cold' was guessed correctly. 'head' (distractor) was guessed, turn ended.
    *   Remaining Team Words: pit, hand, comic, fence, mint, chest, olympus, stadium.
    *   Opponent Words: amazon, kid, bolt, ground, post, skyscraper, horseshoe, green.
    *   Distractor Words: knife, crown, ship, slip, rock, cricket.
    *   Kill Word: alien.

2.  **Evaluate Remaining Team Words:**
    *   pit
    *   hand
    *   comic
    *   fence
    *   mint
    *   chest
    *   olympus
    *   stadium

3.  **Brainstorm Connections:**
    *   *olympus, stadium, pit, fence, hand, chest, comic, mint*
    *   **olympus**: Mount Olympus, gods, Greek, mountain, peak.
    *   **stadium**: sports, arena, field, seat, crowd, olympus (Olympic stadium?).
    *   **pit**: hole, trap, orchestra pit, snake pit, pit bull, armpit.
    *   **hand**: body part, card hand, help, clock hand, handshake.
    *   **comic**: book, strip, funny, hero, Marvel/DC.
    *   **fence**: barrier, picket, wooden, fencing (sport), sit on the fence.
    *   **mint**: candy, money, plant, condition (mint condition), mint leaf.
    *   **chest**: body part, box, treasure, storage.

    *   *Potential Group 1: Body Parts*
        *   hand, chest, (pit? armpit), (head - already used/guessed as distractor).
        *   Risk: 'head' was already guessed as a distractor. Is it still on the board? The prompt says "Remaining distractor words are: knife, crown, ship, slip, rock, cricket." So 'head' is removed from the board (already revealed).
        *   Connection: hand, chest, pit (armpit).
        *   Opponent/Distractor check: 'kid' (opp), 'bolt' (opp), 'ground' (opp), 'post' (opp), 'skyscraper' (opp), 'horseshoe' (opp), 'green' (opp). 'knife' (dist), 'crown' (dist), 'ship' (dist), 'slip' (dist), 'rock' (dist), 'cricket' (dist). 'alien' (kill).
        *   'body' connects hand, chest, pit.
        *   Does 'body' connect to opponent words? 'ground' (body ground? no), 'post' (body post? no), 'kid' (body kid? no).
        *   Does 'body' connect to distractors? 'knife' (body knife? no), 'crown' (body crown? head crown), 'ship' (body ship? no), 'slip' (slip body? no), 'rock' (rock body? no), 'cricket' (cricket body? yes, insects have bodies, but is it a strong association compared to human body?).

        *   'chest' and 'hand' are strong body parts.
        *   What about 'arm'? hand, pit (armpit), chest (nearby).
        *   What about 'upper'? upper hand, upper chest, upper pit? No.

    *   *Potential Group 2: Sports/Venues*
        *   stadium, olympus (Olympics), pit (pit stop? racing?), fence (fencing?), hand (handball?).
        *   Connection: 'olympic'.
        *   Targets: olympus, stadium.
        *   Maybe 'fence' (fencing is an Olympic sport).

        *   Risk: Opponent has 'bolt' (Usain Bolt - Olympics), 'ground' (cricket ground/football ground), 'horseshoe' (horseshoe pitching?).
        *   'bolt' is a strong risk for 'olympic' or 'stadium'. Usain Bolt is synonymous with Olympics.

        'MEMBER' is less risky.
        'ORGAN' is less risky.
        'LIMB' is less risky.

        What about 'hand', 'chest', 'mint'?
        'Palm'. (Hand, Mint - Palm Mint?, Chest - Palm Chest?).
        'Glove'. (Hand, Chest - glove compartment?, Mint - glove mint?).
        'Box'. (Chest, Mint - box of mints, Comic - box set).
        Risks: 'post' (mailbox), 'ship' (box ship), 'knife' (box knife).
        'Box' is very risky.

kid, hand, horseshoe, chest, post, green, fence, ground, cold, alien, slip, knife, ship, crown, pit, cricket, skyscraper, rock, head, comic, mint, olympus, amazon, stadium, bolt
GUESS: cold, head, chest

The word cold was guessed correctly. The word head was guessed but is an innocent word. Your turn ended there.
Now provide another comma-separated list of at least 1 and up to 2 words from the following list of words that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'panel'. Remember to start your answer with 'GUESS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the list compared to previous requests.

kid, hand, horseshoe, chest, green, fence, ground, alien, slip, knife, ship, crown, pit, cricket, skyscraper, rock, comic, mint, olympus, amazon, stadium, bolt

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: pit, hand, comic, fence, mint, chest, olympus, cold, stadium.
Your opponent's team words are: amazon, kid, bolt, ground, post, skyscraper, horseshoe, green.
Distractor words are: knife, crown, head, ship, slip, rock, cricket.
The kill words are: alien.

CLUE: HEALTH
TARGETS: cold, chest, mint

The word cold was guessed correctly. The word head was guessed but is an innocent word. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: pit, hand, comic, fence, mint, chest, olympus, stadium.
Remaining words for your opponent are: amazon, kid, bolt, ground, post, skyscraper, horseshoe, green.
Remaining distractor words are: knife, crown, ship, slip, rock, cricket.
Remaining kill words are: alien.

CLUE: PANEL
TARGETS: comic, fence

The words comic, fence were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. Notice: some words have been removed from the lists compared to previous requests.

Your remaining team words are: pit, hand, mint, chest, olympus, stadium.
Remaining words for your opponent are: amazon, kid, bolt, ground, skyscraper, horseshoe, green.
Remaining distractor words are: knife, crown, ship, slip, rock, cricket.
Remaining kill words are: alien.

CLUE: BODY
TARGETS: hand, chest, pit

The words hand, chest were guessed correctly. Your teammate's turn ended there.
Now provide another clue relating to some of your remaining team words and a list of the related target words. Remember to start your clue with 'CLUE: ', put a new line, and start your comma-separated list of target words with 'TARGETS: '. manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.

The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.
